message: feat: add Build Slot, Human Overseer, and Dual-Mode visualizations

files.patch:
@@ -45,6 +45,9 @@ const TuiScreensViz = dynamic(() => import("@/components/viz/tui-screens-viz"), const RobotModeViz = dynamic(() => import("@/components/viz/robot-mode-viz"), { ssr: false }); const ProductBusViz = dynamic(() => import("@/components/viz/product-bus-viz"), { ssr: false }); const ReliabilityInternalsViz = dynamic(() => import("@/components/viz/reliability-internals-viz"), { ssr: false }); +const BuildSlotCoordinatorViz = dynamic(() => import("@/components/viz/build-slot-coordinator-viz"), { ssr: false }); +const HumanOverseerViz = dynamic(() => import("@/components/viz/human-overseer-viz"), { ssr: false }); +const DualModeInterfaceViz = dynamic(() => import("@/components/viz/dual-mode-interface-viz"), { ssr: false }); function VizLoader() { return ( @@ -135,6 +138,11 @@ export default function ShowcasePage() {

To prevent an adversarial or uncoordinated swarm from spamming each other, Agent Mail enforces a strict handshake. + The visualization now includes both approval and rejection branches so you can compare downstream behavior. +

+

+ Watch for the state boundary where permission flips from blocked to trusted. That transition is + explicit, logged, and queryable, which is critical for post-incident reasoning.

@@ -160,6 +168,10 @@ export default function ShowcasePage() {

Before editing a file, an agent requests a lock via MCP. If another agent holds it, they get an advisory conflict warning.

+

+ Step through enforce, warn, and bypass guard modes to see how policy changes commit/push outcomes + without hiding ownership intent or stale-lock recovery mechanics. +

@@ -184,6 +196,10 @@ export default function ShowcasePage() {

By passing the issue ID to reservation requests, the entire swarm knows exactly why a file is locked.

+

+ In discovered-work mode, the demo shows how newly uncovered tasks link back to the parent bead + instead of becoming disconnected TODO noise. +

@@ -208,6 +224,9 @@ export default function ShowcasePage() {

Under the hood, Agent Mail acts as an MCP server providing tools over stdio/HTTP. It persists everything locally to SQLite.

+

+ Use the mode toggles to compare message, reservation, and search request paths on the same architecture diagram. +

@@ -232,6 +251,9 @@ export default function ShowcasePage() {

Search V3 parses queries, routes them through lexical and semantic tiers with budget-derived candidate caps, fuses results via RRF, and applies field-match and recency reranking. When tiers fail, the pipeline gracefully degrades to a chronological fallback scan.

+

+ Keep the same query preset while switching modes to see exactly how recall, relevance, and latency shift. +

@@ -258,6 +280,10 @@ export default function ShowcasePage() { and surfaces the primary signals needed for operators to make fast decisions. Jump keys provide O(1) navigation to any screen.

+

+ Filter by category, then inspect the selected screen's core question to understand the information + architecture logic behind every jump key. +

@@ -284,6 +310,9 @@ export default function ShowcasePage() { The toon format minimizes token usage, JSON provides full data fidelity for programmatic use, and Markdown renders threads and messages for human-readable contexts.

+

+ The command recipe panel demonstrates a practical sequence agents can run to triage, decide, and report. +

@@ -310,6 +339,9 @@ export default function ShowcasePage() { agents can search across all repos, send messages to agents in other projects using name@project addressing, and coordinate via contact governance policies.

+

+ The stepper now makes the governance order explicit: contact policy resolution precedes cross-project messaging. +

@@ -344,6 +376,87 @@ export default function ShowcasePage() { collection, and integrity checking — each recover gracefully from stalls without blocking the others.

+

+ Toggle between normal and stress profiles to see how queue pressure and commit latency move under load. +

+ + + + + {/* ================================================================ + 0.10 Dual-Mode Interface Safety + ================================================================ */} + +
+ + }> + + + +
+

+ If a coding agent accidentally executes the human operator CLI (am), + the system immediately exits with code 2. This prevents the LLM from burning thousands of tokens trying to parse and interact with + ANSI escape sequences in an interactive terminal application. +

+
+
+ + + {/* ================================================================ + 0.11 Build Slot Coordination + ================================================================ */} + +
+ + }> + + + +
+

+ When multiple agents attempt to compile the same codebase concurrently, it leads to massive performance degradation and lock conflicts. + The acquire_build_slot tool allows agents to request mutually exclusive runtime + leases on critical computational resources. +

+
+
+ + + {/* ================================================================ + 0.12 Human Overseer Injection + ================================================================ */} + +
+ + }> + + + +
+

+ Traditional prompt engineering requires killing the agent session to provide feedback. + The Human Overseer mechanism injects messages with importance: urgent + that bypass all standard contact policies. This allows operators to seamlessly pause, pivot, or unblock autonomous swarms mid-execution. +

@@ -0,0 +1,75 @@ +"use client"; + +import { VizSurface } from "@/components/viz/viz-framework"; +import { Terminal, ShieldX, CheckCircle, SplitSquareVertical } from "lucide-react"; + +export default function DualModeInterfaceViz() { + return ( + +
+

Strict Dual-Mode Safety

+

+ Agent Mail uses a shared core binary but enforces strict runtime surface separation to prevent disastrous mode-confusion loops. +

+
+ +
+ {/* Left Side: Agent Context */} +
+
+

+ + Agent Context (MCP) +

+ +
+
+
+ $ mcp-agent-mail +
+
Starts the stdio JSON-RPC server cleanly.
+
+ +
+
+ $ am status +
+
+ BLOCKED: Agent tried to run the human CLI! Fails with Exit Code 2 to prevent hallucinating an infinite interactive TUI loop. +
+
+
+
+
+ + {/* Right Side: Human Context */} +
+
+

+ + Operator Context (TTY) +

+ +
+
+
+ $ am status +
+
Boots the interactive 15-screen TUI correctly.
+
+ +
+
+ $ mcp-agent-mail +
+
+ BLOCKED: Human tried to run the raw MCP server in a TTY! Fails with a friendly warning to use am. +
+
+
+
+
+
+ + ); +}
